title: Distributed lock for singleton job execution
scenario_context: Multiple instances run cron. Without lock, job runs N times.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Lock without TTL — deadlock on crash
reasoning: SET NX with TTL. Lock owner can unlock. TTL covers crashes.
edge_cases_addressed: TTL too short — job continues without lock
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) NX + TTL. 2) Owner-only release via Lua. 3) Crash-safe.

title: Typed event emitter for app-wide events
scenario_context: Pub/sub between modules. Naive: string event names, any payload.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Typo in event name — silent miss.
reasoning: Map of event → payload type. Typed on/emit.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Typed contract. 2) Cleanup. 3) Per-scope where needed.

title: Tenant-bound storage paths (S3 prefix)
scenario_context: User uploads files. Storage paths must prevent cross-tenant access via URL guess.
reasoning: Path: tenants/<id>/<random-uuid>.ext. All access via signed URL with TTL.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Prefix isolation. 2) Signed + TTL. 3) Path verification at access.

title: Annual prepayment with refund on cancel
scenario_context: User pays annual; cancels in month 3. Prorated refund. Need: clear calculation + UX.
reasoning: Stripe handles via prorate on cancel. Preview shows refund amount + date.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Preview before cancel. 2) Stripe prorate. 3) Email confirmation.

title: Onboarding analytics: funnel events
scenario_context: PM needs funnel drop-off per onboarding step. Need consistent event taxonomy.
reasoning: const EVENTS = { ONBOARDING_STEP_STARTED: 'onboarding.step.started', ... }. Type-safe emit.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Constants. 2) Typed properties. 3) Consistent taxonomy.

title: OAuth scopes mapped to internal permissions
scenario_context: Third-party integration requests OAuth scopes. Map to our permission system.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Integration with all-access; user didn't realize.
reasoning: Scopes like 'read:projects', 'write:tasks'. Consent screen. Token carries scopes; middleware checks per endpoint.
frontend_solution: // consent screen with explicit scope list
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Consent transparent. 2) Mapping to internal. 3) Granular.

title: Recharts ScatterChart with size by dimension
scenario_context: Bubble-style scatter: x, y, size, color.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User can't tell what size means.
reasoning: ZAxis with range. Tooltip shows all 4 dimensions. Size legend.
frontend_solution: // <ScatterChart><XAxis dataKey="x" /><YAxis dataKey="y" /><ZAxis dataKey="size" range={[50, 500]} /><Scatter data={...} fill="#3b82f6" /></ScatterChart>
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) ZAxis area-mapped. 2) Tooltip rich. 3) Size legend.

title: Visx area difference with reference baseline
scenario_context: Show deviation from target. Filled area between current and target.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Two lines without fill — story unclear
reasoning: Area between value and target, colored by sign (above/below).
frontend_solution: // client/components/AreaDifference.tsx (using visx AreaClosed + clip path for above/below)
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Sign-based color. 2) Filled emphasis. 3) Legend.

title: Chart.js financial chart (OHLC) via custom controller
scenario_context: Candlestick chart. Chart.js doesn't ship; use plugin or custom controller.
reasoning: chartjs-chart-financial. Bullish green, bearish red. Volume sub-chart.
frontend_solution:
// import { FinancialController, CandlestickController, CandlestickElement } from 'chartjs-chart-financial';
// Chart.register(FinancialController, CandlestickController, CandlestickElement);
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Established plugin. 2) Conventional colors. 3) Volume context.

title: ECharts radar with multi-product comparison
scenario_context: Compare 3-5 products on 6+ axes. ECharts radar shape comparison.
reasoning: Per-axis max in indicator. Limit to ≤4 series.
frontend_solution: // option = { radar: { indicator: axes.map(a => ({ name: a.name, max: a.max })) }, series: [{ type: 'radar', data: products }] };
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Per-axis max. 2) Limit series. 3) Raw + normalized.

title: Tremor Toggle for time-range presets
scenario_context: Day/Week/Month/Year toggle. Tremor TabGroup is right primitive.
reasoning: Shared TabGroup. Translates to from/to dates consistently.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Shared component. 2) Consistent date math. 3) URL.

title: Animation: meaningful, never decorative
scenario_context: Animations should communicate change, not show off.
verification_chain: Layer 8: animation on data change only
reasoning: Animations < 300ms. Only on data change (not initial). Disabled when reduced motion.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Communicative not decorative. 2) Short. 3) Respect preference.

title: Adding webhook with delivery test
scenario_context: User adds webhook URL. Naive: save and hope. Better: test delivery before saving as active.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User saves URL with typo; never knows webhooks fail.
reasoning: On save, fire test event with signed payload. Show result. Only mark verified after success.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Verify before save. 2) Test == real format. 3) Surface result.

title: What if disk fills mid-write — abort + alert
scenario_context: Server disk fills. Writes fail. App degrades.
reasoning: Monitor disk. Auto-rotate logs. Reject writes when above threshold. Read-only mode possible.
backend_solution: // /healthz includes disk check; alert via metric
test_strategy: Fill disk in test; assert handling
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Monitor + alert. 2) Graceful degrade. 3) Rotate.

title: Type-safe i18n with key validation
scenario_context: Translations spread across components. Typos in keys silently miss.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Missing key returns key (visible to user)
what_breaks_naive_approach: Typo in key → 'users.tiitle' → English user sees that.
reasoning: Generate type from en.json keys. t() typed.
frontend_solution:
// type Keys = keyof typeof enJson;
// function t(key: Keys, vars?: Record<string, string>): string { ... }
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Compile-time check. 2) Fallback. 3) Plural-aware.

title: Tenant-level webhook delivery dashboard
scenario_context: Tenant admin needs to see webhook delivery health.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Tenant can't tell why webhooks broken.
reasoning: Delivery log table. Dashboard: per-endpoint stats + recent failures + replay button.
edge_cases_addressed: PII in payload — show meta only
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Tenant self-serve debug. 2) Replay button. 3) Recent failures highlighted.

title: Coupon stacking rules
scenario_context: Some coupons stack (loyalty + holiday), others don't (free trial + discount). Need rule engine.
verification_chain: Layer 1: coupon table with stackable flag
reasoning: Stackable list per coupon. On apply, check compatibility with existing.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Explicit rules. 2) Per-coupon. 3) Clear conflict UX.

title: Onboarding checklist with progress
scenario_context: Show '3 of 7 done' style checklist that persists.
reasoning: Per-user state. Collapsible (default open until N items done). Skippable items.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Progress motivates. 2) Collapsible respects autonomy. 3) Persistent.

title: Workspace ownership transfer
scenario_context: Owner leaves. Transfer workspace to another admin. Need: confirmation, dual-side accept, audit.
reasoning: Step 1: owner initiates. Step 2: target accepts. Email notification. Billing stays with workspace (target adds card).
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Two-step. 2) Audit. 3) Expiry.

title: Recharts custom legend with interactions
scenario_context: Click legend item toggles series; hover highlights.
reasoning: Custom Legend content. Track hidden state. Apply hide to Line.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Custom legend ergonomic. 2) aria-pressed. 3) Reset.

title: Visx chord diagram for flow relationships
scenario_context: Migration flow between regions. Chord shows pairwise volume.
reasoning: d3-chord computes arcs + ribbons. Hover dims unrelated.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Chord for pairwise. 2) Hover focus. 3) Labels readable.

title: Chart.js area chart with smooth curves
scenario_context: Tension parameter for smooth lines. Too high → misleads.
reasoning: tension <=0.4. cubicInterpolationMode: 'monotone' for monotone-preserving.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Conservative tension. 2) Monotone where appropriate. 3) Honest visual.

title: ECharts boxplot for distribution
scenario_context: Compare distributions across categories.
reasoning: Server returns [min, q1, median, q3, max] per category. Outliers separate. ECharts boxplot.
frontend_solution: // option = { series: [{ type: 'boxplot', data: quartilesByCategory }, { type: 'scatter', data: outliers }] };
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Server quartiles. 2) Outliers explicit. 3) Distribution > average.

title: Tremor scrollable Donut with clickable segments
scenario_context: Donut + list of segments. Click segment highlights.
reasoning: DonutChart + scrollable Card with rows. Selected highlight syncs both.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Two views one source. 2) Scrollable list. 3) Selection sync.

title: Chart legend wrapping on mobile
scenario_context: Many series legend overflows mobile. Wrap or scroll?
reasoning: flex-wrap on legend. Collapse to button 'N more' on tap.
frontend_solution: // Custom legend with flex-wrap and 'show more'
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Mobile-first. 2) Progressive disclosure. 3) Tap targets.

title: Adding background job: queue + status + retry
scenario_context: Heavy report generation. Naive: blocks request. Better: queue, status endpoint, UI polls.
reasoning: BullMQ or pg-boss. Status endpoint returns progress. UI polls. Retry on failure.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Async. 2) Persistent status. 3) Worker resilience.

title: Pre-warm cache before traffic
scenario_context: After deploy, cache cold. First requests slow. Need warm-up.
reasoning: Lifecycle hook runs warm-up: fetch top dashboards, cache. Measure cold vs warm.
backend_solution: // post-deploy hook calls /warm endpoint or internal fn
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Lifecycle hook. 2) Top-N priorities. 3) Measured.

title: Strict TS config for catching bugs
scenario_context: Loose tsconfig hides bugs. Strict catches at compile.
reasoning: strict: true + noUncheckedIndexedAccess + noImplicitOverride + exactOptionalPropertyTypes.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Strict from start. 2) noUncheckedIndexedAccess catches off-by-one. 3) No ts-ignore.

title: Tenant data residency
scenario_context: EU customer requires data in EU. Region per tenant.
reasoning: Tenant has region. DNS or path routes to region-specific cluster. Each cluster isolated.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Hard separation. 2) Compliance-first design. 3) Migration playbook.

title: Usage-based pricing tier transitions
scenario_context: Crossing usage threshold moves to higher tier mid-cycle.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User hit hard ceiling, surprised by invoice.
reasoning: Tracking against tier thresholds. Warn at 80%/100%. Auto-tier-up or block per plan setting. Show projection.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Warning + projection. 2) Auto vs block per plan. 3) Pro-rata.
